summary: Implemented substantial item decompilation work for item files it_27CF and it_2E6A, replacing many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations and empty stubs with typed C functions, state tables, spawn logic, item variable structs, callback handling, and lifecycle/update behavior. The PR also improved cross-system typing by updating grmaterial wrappers to pass Ground*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, and HSD_GObjEvent into it_802E6AEC instead of raw ints.
what_changed: src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.c was expanded from placeholder comments/stubs into implemented item behavior, including ItemStateTable it_803F94A0 and functions it_802F2F34, it_802F3020, it_802F3078, it_802F3120, it_802F3150, it_802F317C, it_802F31B8, it_802F3260, it_802F3264, it_802F3290, it_802F32F8, it_802F3330, and it_802F3374.

it_802F2F34 now builds a SpawnItem from it_27CF_DatAttrs, spawns the item with Item_80268B18, copies dat attributes into item fields such as xDD4_itemVar.it_27CF.x0 and xC98, sets lifetime with it_80275158, and transitions through it_802F3020.

it_27CF lifecycle functions now decrement xD44_lifeTimer, spawn an effect through efAsync_Spawn when the timer expires, transition to state 2 with Item_80268E5C, reset velocity, and handle fall/collision-related callbacks.

src/melee/it/items/it_2E6A.c was filled from an effectively empty file into a 242-line item implementation with static ItemAttr it_803F8C08, ItemStateTable it_803F8C8C, scale constant it_803B8730, item spawn/setup function it_802E6AEC, update callback it_802E6D60, event callbacks it_802E6F7C and it_802E6FC0, touched callback it_802E7054, and cleanup/reference helper it_802E709C.

it_802E6AEC now takes typed inputs including Ground*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, HSD_GObjEvent, creates a Pokemon_Random item, derives the spawn position either from a JObj via lb_8000B1CC or from an explicit Vec3, writes item variables for either JObj-attached or position-attached modes, applies a scale, sets collision/touch behavior, and stores callback-like fields.
it_802E6D60 updates item position, scale, and rotation from a referenced HSD_JObj when item variable mode x2 equals 1, and reports an ityaku.c error with an infinite loop for unexpected mode values.

itCommonItems.h gained it_27CF_ItemVars, it_27CF_DatAttrs, it_2E6A_ItemVars_1, and it_2E6A_ItemVars_2, and added comments documenting suspected field purposes such as lifetime, item var x0, item->xC98, and ItemKind.
it_27CF.h replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for the newly implemented functions with concrete signatures using HSD_GObj*, Item_GObj*, it_27CF_DatAttrs*, bool, and void return types.

it_2E6A.h changed it_802E6AEC from a raw all-int prototype to a typed Item_GObj* returning prototype with Ground*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, and HSD_GObjEvent parameters, and changed it_802E709C's second parameter from Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj*.

grmaterial.c wrapper functions grMaterial_801C8CFC and grMaterial_801C8D44 were retargeted from raw int arguments to typed Ground*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, and HSD_GObjEvent arguments when forwarding into it_802E6AEC.
types.h was updated to support the new item variable layouts, though the provided excerpt does not show the exact full change.
smart_moves: The PR pushed type information outward from the item implementation into headers and callers, so grmaterial wrappers and item headers now document real pointer/event parameter types instead of preserving misleading int-only prototypes.
The implementation separates two observed layouts for it_2E6A item variables, one storing an HSD_JObj* and one storing a Vec3, rather than forcing a single uncertain struct too early.

The code preserved uncertainty with inline comments such as questions about whether it_2F28 and it_27CF should share structs and whether it_2E6A structs can be combined, which is useful for later cleanup without hiding unresolved layout questions.
State table reconstruction was done alongside function prototype recovery, giving future readers a concrete map from item states to anim/physics/collision callbacks.

Matching-oriented artifacts were retained in comments and code, including PAD_STACK usage, commented alternate signatures, explicit casts between Item_GObj* and HSD_GObj*, and a disabled inline-sharing experiment for duplicated timer/effect logic.
The item spawn setup mirrors existing engine patterns: fill SpawnItem fields, call Item_80268B18, use GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ, set lifetime/state/flags, and assign item callbacks.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled item is driven by stage or ground code, update the caller-facing wrapper prototypes at the same time as the item function; otherwise raw int prototypes obscure Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_JObj*, and HSD_GObjEvent relationships.

Item xDD4 item variable regions may represent different union overlays depending on mode flags. It can be safer to model separate candidate structs and document the discriminant than to prematurely merge layouts.

For item lifecycle functions, xD44_lifeTimer, xCA0, item velocity, Item_80268E5C state transitions, and efAsync_Spawn calls are recurring anchors for matching behavior and identifying state transitions.
State table entries are valuable evidence for function signatures: callbacks that return bool or void can often be inferred from their use in ItemStateTable slots and item engine callback APIs.
Retaining known-source strings such as OSReport("%s:%d: oioi...", "ityaku.c", 0xD7) can help identify original file names or behavior while the symbol remains address-based.

Function pointer fields stored in item vars should be cast narrowly at call sites when struct typing is not yet known, but this should be treated as an interim decomp tactic rather than final semantic naming.
SpawnItem initialization details matter for matching and behavior: prev_pos/pos, facing_dir, x3C_damage, parent gobj fields, x44_flag.b0, x40, and vel are all explicitly reconstructed here.
naming_conventions: Address-based item filenames and symbols were retained: it_27CF, it_2E6A, it_802F2F34, it_802E6AEC, etc.
Recovered data layouts were named with the pattern it_<address>_ItemVars and it_<address>_DatAttrs, for example it_27CF_ItemVars, it_27CF_DatAttrs, it_2E6A_ItemVars_1, and it_2E6A_ItemVars_2.
Struct field names remain offset-like or provisional where semantics are weak, such as x0, x2, x4, x10, x14, x18, x1C, float1, float2, float3, and x24.
Comments attach tentative semantics to anonymous fields rather than renaming aggressively, e.g. float1 as lifetime, float3 as item var x0, x24 as item->xC98, and x28 as ItemKind.
The code uses existing project object aliases and casts between HSD_GObj* and Item_GObj* rather than introducing a new item object abstraction.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in timer callbacks, with PAD_STACK(10) in the active it_802F3078 and it_802F31B8 versions and a disabled PAD_STACK(8) inline attempt.
Kept an #if 0 block experimenting with an inline helper for duplicated timer/effect behavior, indicating a matching attempt where shared inline code may not have produced the desired output.
Retained commented alternate signatures such as Item_GObj* versus HSD_GObj* for several functions to document signature pressure from matching/call sites.
Used explicit casts for callback and object-type mismatches, including Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj*, and void* item-var fields to function pointer types.
Modeled an original assertion/fatal path with OSReport followed by an infinite goto loop in it_802E6D60, likely to match compiler output for an unrecoverable unexpected state.
Initialized static data such as ItemAttr it_803F8C08 and Vec3 it_803B8730 directly in C, including comments about uncertain or missing ItemAttr fields.
Set it_804D6D38[spawn.kind - 0x2B]->x0_common_attr = &it_803F8C08 before spawning, a noteworthy tactic for matching item common attribute replacement behavior.
review_feedback: The only human text in the provided context is a short merge/comment from ribbanya: "Sorry for taking so long on this. Thanks for your continued efforts."
No review comments or requested code changes are present in the provided PR slice.
Because there are no detailed reviewer comments in the slice, there is no evidence of specific reviewer intent about the uncertain structs, signatures, or matching tactics.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching it_2E6A_ItemVars_1 or it_2E6A_ItemVars_2 to see whether the two layouts were merged or renamed.
Search for later cleanup of it_27CF_DatAttrs field names float1, float2, float3, x24, and x28.
Search for later changes to it_802E6AEC signature or callback field types x14, x18, and x1C.
Look for PRs that remove the #if 0 inline experiment in it_27CF.c or adjust PAD_STACK values in it_802F3078 and it_802F31B8.
Investigate whether grMaterial_801C8CFC and grMaterial_801C8D44 receive further typed naming for their remaining int parameters.
Search for ItemAttr it_803F8C08 cleanup to confirm the uncertain x84-x9C comment and field mapping.
